ote:

>>> ./devel/py-celery
>>> ./devel/py-TGScheduler
>>>
>>> All the other do not have strict dependency on 1.5 (or just define !2.0=
)
>>> or do not need dateutil at all like devel/py-mwlib (patch sent to
>>> maintainer). Please fix two of your ports and I will deal with
>>> py-TGScheduler. That's will be all that's needed :)
>>
>>
>>

>> maintain that one as well). However I was waiting for ports/175372
>> before doing so.
>
>
> Hmm, indeed, sorry. I'll contact celery maintainer then. But why to not
> update the patch from ports/175372 to include datetime change? It will br=
eak
> at tinderbox check in it's current state... And it's now time to ping cul=
ot@
> about the PR, because he seems wait for dependent ports/172898 that was

Will do, thanks.
